title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied of Reading, Mass., sentenced to eight months for trafficking 100+ endangered animal parts.
Bied ordered animal parts from dealers in Cameroon and Indonesia, who hunted protected species per his instructions.
He hid imports by labeling them as 'decorative masks' and was caught selling leopard skulls to an undercover agent.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black was convicted of first-degree murder for the fatal stabbing of Milteer Hendricks in 2023.
Hendricks, 19, was stabbed outside a Falmouth apartment building on East Falmouth Highway and died the next day.
Black faces a mandatory life sentence without parole, with sentencing scheduled for Friday.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts State Police Lt. Anthony DeLucia suspended with pay amid investigation into Sgt. Scott Quigley's alleged drunk driving crash.
Quigley indicted for negligent homicide; accused of crashing into vehicle, leading to Angelo Schettino's death in December 2023.
DeLucia allegedly advised DA's director to keep Quigley's intoxication details secret, sparking suspension and inquiry.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker sentenced to 3-5 years for manslaughter of 10-month-old foster child.
The couple ignored signs the child needed treatment, leading to death from treatable infections, prosecutors report.
Sentences are staggered due to their four other children; neither can serve in caregiving roles again.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, sentenced to life without parole for killing boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in 2022.
Church was convicted of first-degree murder after toxicology reports showed Fowler died from ethylene glycol poisoning.
Prosecutors claimed Church poisoned Fowler's drink due to jealousy over his affair; she plans to appeal her conviction.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox have started the 2026 season 1-5, their worst start since 2019.
Boston's pitching struggles, with a team ERA of 4.91, and ranks last in home runs allowed per nine innings.
Key offensive players like Roman Anthony and Trevor Story are underperforming, contributing to the team's overall batting woes.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, 35, announces retirement from the NFL after 13 seasons, with notable stints at the Bills and Patriots.
Gilmore won Super Bowl LIII with the Patriots and was named NFL Defensive Player of the Year in 2019.
During his career, Gilmore achieved 617 tackles, 149 pass deflections, and 32 interceptions, including two postseason picks.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle has begun global layoffs with employees receiving termination notices, but the total scale and impact remain unclear.
Analysts estimate Oracle's layoffs could affect 20,000 to 30,000 workers, approximately 18% of its workforce.
Massachusetts filings show no Oracle layoffs yet, despite offices in Burlington and Nashua being potential sites.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year contract with the Cincinnati Bengals, marking his third team in three years.
Dugger was traded from the Patriots to the Steelers for a sixth-round pick due to decreased playing time.
Dugger recorded 42 tackles, 2 interceptions, and a sack in nine games with the Steelers last season.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A family's truck was stolen from Logan Airport's central parking while they were on vacation.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating, noting similar theft at the same garage weeks earlier.
Stolen vehicle was tracked to New Jersey; investigation ongoing with no arrests made.
